title: rumex verticillatus subsp. fascicularis
intro: Rumex verticillatus subsp. fascicularis, also known as the water dock or narrow-leaved dock, is a subspecies of the Rumex verticillatus plant, a perennial herb native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of inflammation, diarrhea, and skin conditions, due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The health benefits of Rumex verticillatus subsp. fascicularis include its ability to reduce swelling, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, with the roots being more medicinal than the leaves.
